methods.method_name: XCT Technique for Wood Density Analysis
methods.description: The XCT technique involves the mathematical reconstruction of the inner three-dimensional structure of an object based on X-ray absorption measurements. This method is used to determine the physical density of wood samples by analyzing X-ray density in Hounsfield Units (HU).

steps.description: Perform tomographic scanning of the reference wood sample with known density.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct tomographic scanning of reference wood sample

steps.description: Calculate X-ray density using the equation with linear attenuation coefficients for the material, water, and air.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate X-ray density using attenuation coefficients

steps.description: Determine the physical density of wood based on XCT results.
steps.searchable_summary: determine wood density from XCT results

methods.description: A numerical experiment was conducted to determine the necessary sample size for measuring dendrometric characteristics using virtual cores extracted from X-ray tomograms.

steps.description: Extract a model 3D core from X-ray tomogram of a whole wood sample.
Analyze virtual cores of different diameters to determine statistically significant sample size.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze virtual cores for sample size determination

methods.description: Mathematical processing of scan data and digital image processing were performed using various software tools to analyze and visualize the data.

steps.description: Perform mathematical processing of scan data using MATLAB R2017b and ISee 1.11.1.
steps.searchable_summary: process scan data with MATLAB and ISee

steps.description: Conduct digital image processing using Adobe Photoshop CS6.
Carry out numerical analysis and data visualization using OriginPro 9.1 and CTVox 3.3.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize data with OriginPro and CTVox
